name: Oysters Rockefeller (Gourmet Mag.)
recipeIngredient: 1 head Boston lettuce, washed, dried, and chopped finely
1/2 pound fresh spinach, washed, dried, and chopped finely
recipeInstructions:
1. Shuck the oysters and reserve the liquor and bottom shells.
2. Scrub and dry the reserved shells.
3. In a bowl, combine chopped lettuce, spinach, scallion, 1/2 cup bread crumbs, parsley, celery, and minced garlic.
4. In a skillet, melt butter over moderate heat. Cook the spinach mixture, stirring for 1-2 minutes or until wilted. Stir in Pernod, anchovy paste, cayenne pepper, and salt and pepper to taste. Chill the mixture, covered, for 1 hour.
5. In another skillet, cook bacon over moderate heat until crisp. Drain on paper towels and crumble.
6. Arrange one oyster in each of the reserved shells and moisten with some of the reserved liquor. Spread half of the spinach mixture onto the oysters using heaping tablespoons. Sprinkle bacon over each oyster.
7. Top each oyster with the remaining spinach mixture and sprinkle with 1 tablespoon of remaining bread crumbs.
8. Arrange the oysters on an oven-safe platter filled with coarse salt.
9. Bake in a preheated 450°F (230°C) oven for 18 minutes or until the bread crumbs are well browned.
10. Garnish with parsley sprigs and lemon wedges, and serve.
